skill.name: building-plugins
skill.description: Guides creation, validation, documentation, and distribution of Claude Code plugins that bundle agents, skills, commands, hooks, and helper scripts.
skill.summary: Create and validate complete Claude Code plugin packages.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, secret, and critical deletion patterns. Context review found the most severe hits are documentation examples or defensive checks, not confirmed malicious behavior. The skill still has medium risk because it uses broad Bash and file-editing permissions and ships plugin templates with executable script workflows.

medium_findings.title: Broad File and Bash Tooling for Plugin Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Write, and Edit permissions and is designed to create plugin directories, manifests, commands, hooks, and scripts. This is legitimate for a plugin-building skill, but it gives generated workflows meaningful filesystem and command execution reach.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission declaration and plugin creation workflow are explicit. The risk is capability exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Executable Template Scripts Included
medium_findings.description: The skill ships shell templates for hook validation and helper commands. The reviewed scripts quote user input and mostly perform validation, but generated or copied scripts can affect user projects and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable scripts are real files in the distributed template. The current script bodies appear benign, so the confidence is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Static Command Flags Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are markdown fences, directory trees, slash-command examples, or installation snippets. They are not executed by the skill during audit review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited ranges are fenced documentation examples or installation instructions. No evidence found that these snippets run automatically.

low_findings.title: Dangerous rm and eval Patterns Are Defensive Checks
low_findings.description: The critical recursive-delete and eval detections appear in security guidance or validation logic that warns about or blocks dangerous patterns. No evidence found that the skill executes rm -rf / or eval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly searches for or blocks dangerous text patterns. The SKILL.md occurrence is a direct instruction to avoid dynamic execution.

low_findings.title: Secret and URL Flags Are Placeholder Guidance
low_findings.description: API key and URL detections are examples for plugin manifests, author metadata, or secret-management guidance. No hardcoded real credential or exfiltration endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited values are placeholders such as API_KEY, email@example.com, and GitHub profile examples. No evidence found of real secret collection or transmission.

low_findings.title: Manual Symlink Instructions Touch Home Directory
low_findings.description: The documentation recommends symlinking plugin folders into the Claude plugin directory. This is expected installation behavior, but it modifies a hidden directory under the user home when followed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The symlink instruction is clearly present and targets ~/.claude/plugins. It is normal plugin installation guidance, so severity remains low.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Permission in Skill Metadata
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash as an allowed tool. This is expected for validation and scaffolding, but it increases impact if prompts or generated templates are misused.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission is visible in the skill front matter. The risk comes from broad command capability, not from a confirmed malicious command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The repository includes shell scripts intended for generated plugins. They should be reviewed before distribution because hooks can run during tool-use events.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are present and executable in purpose. Their current contents are simple and mostly defensive, which limits confidence and severity.

content.value_statement: Creating a complete plugin requires consistent manifests, component layout, validation, and documentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through plugin scaffolding, review, and publishing steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains required and recommended plugin.json fields for Claude Code plugins.
Provides minimal, standard, and full plugin directory templates.
Guides creation of agents, skills, commands, hooks, scripts, and README files.
Includes validation guidance for plugin names, versions, paths, and metadata.
Documents manual, marketplace, and private plugin distribution workflows.
Provides security guidance for permissions, secrets, scripts, and dependencies.
limitations: It does not publish plugins by itself without user action and platform access.
Generated scripts and hooks still need project-specific security review.
Marketplace acceptance depends on external policies and reviewer decisions.
Examples use placeholder names, URLs, and credentials that users must replace.

use_cases.title: Create a New Plugin Package
use_cases.description: Plan and scaffold a plugin that combines related agents, skills, commands, and hooks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Developer Tools
use_cases.description: Package repeatable workflows into a consistent Claude Code plugin for a team.

use_cases.title: Prepare Marketplace Documentation
use_cases.description: Create README content, version guidance, and distribution notes before publication.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Minimal Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a minimal Claude Code plugin named [plugin-name] for [purpose]. Include the required manifest and README structure.

prompt_templates.title: Add Plugin Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a plugin for [workflow]. Recommend which agents, skills, commands, and hooks it should include, with clear responsibilities for each component.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an Existing Plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plugin structure for Claude Code compatibility. Check naming, plugin.json fields, component paths, documentation, and security concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare this plugin for marketplace or private distribution. Review versioning, README quality, installation steps, release notes, and risk warnings.

output_examples.input: Create a plugin for code review workflows.
output: A proposed plugin layout with agents for security and style review.
Recommended commands for targeted reviews and full-project checks.
A documentation checklist for installation, usage, and troubleshooting.
A list of manifest issues, missing documentation, and path problems.
Security notes for scripts, hooks, permissions, and secret handling.
Release readiness guidance with required fixes and optional improvements.

output_examples.input: Prepare marketplace copy for my plugin.
output: A concise plugin summary, feature list, and installation guidance.
Suggested keywords and user-facing use cases.
A maintainer checklist for versioning, support links, and update notes.

best_practices: Keep each plugin component focused on one clear responsibility.
Validate generated scripts, hooks, and manifests before publishing or sharing.
Document required environment variables without storing real credentials.
anti_patterns: Bundling unrelated agents, commands, and hooks into one plugin.
Granting broad tool permissions when a narrower set is enough.
Publishing placeholder URLs, names, or example secrets as real configuration.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Claude Code plugins that package agents, skills, commands, hooks, scripts, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it create plugin.json files?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains required fields, recommended metadata, component paths, and common manifest mistakes.

faq.question: Does it publish plugins automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can prepare distribution guidance, but publishing requires user action and the correct platform access.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses Bash and file editing, and it includes script templates. These capabilities are useful but need review.

faq.question: Can teams use it for private plugins?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for private distribution, internal documentation, and controlled plugin installation.

faq.question: Does it support Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
